rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DOLORES RUBIN LEVIN TRUST DATED DECEMBER 22, 2017 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Jeremy Levin, Jonathan Levin And Elise Patler, As Co-Executors Of The Estate Of Sheldon M. Levin, In Place Of Sheldon M. Levin; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. The unopposed Motion to Substitute Jeremy Levin, Jonathan Levin and Elise Patler, as co-Executors of the Estate of Sheldon M. Levin, in Place of Sheldon M. Levin is granted. Although the motion only cites Code of Civil Procedure section 377.31, it also implicitly invokes Code of Civil Procedure sections 377.40, 377.41 as well. Accordingly, the Motion is granted. The moving party shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, prior to the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on September 14, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925#, participant ID 0#, and passcode 722809#. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRIAN LANSMERE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if the petition should be granted, when (1) no courtesy copy of the supplement filed on 8/20/21 has been received yet by the Examiner (likely as a result of our Court clerks being extremely short-staffed) and (2) there are still issues not fully addressed. 1) A notice mailed to a person in care of another person is insufficient unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of the second person. [CRC 7.51(a)(2).] There is no explanation given for notice mailed to maternal first cousin Vera May Mitchell c/o Alison M. Mayne and no supporting documents showing Alison Mayne's authority to receive such notice. 2) No supporting documentation has been filed to support the lineage of the children of the decedent's purported paternal grandfather Albert L. Zusmer: Decedent's father Mark Lansmere and Mark Lansmere's 10 siblings. The administrator was apparently unable to obtain official documents from abroad and relied on the "family historian" whose (incomplete) chart is also attached to the Paternal Genealogical Chart filed on 6/23/21. Ordinarily, an heirship petition provides supporting documentation for even those predeceased ancestors/heirs who died without issue, including birth, death, and marriage records, if any. 3) There are believed to be five maternal first cousins once removed, for whom the administrator has not been able to obtain any records and an attorney in the UK representing some of the maternal heirs has been unable to reach. There have been no objections to including them as heirs. The administrator requests that they be declared heirs so that the estate can be distributed and their shares set aside (deposited with the county treasurer), pursuant to Prob. C. Sect. 11850. (The fact that someone was in her 50s or 60s and a "spinster" when she died is not proof that she had no issue. See paragraph 3 of the supplement filed 8/27/21.) Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HUGH ELWOOD MACBETH JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/27/2021. Second continuance for same issues. 1) Please explain why the petition alleges Decedent died September 12, 2019. The petition for probate and Order for Probate state the Decedent died September 14, 2019. 2) A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. [LRSF 14.35]. 3) The I&A alleges under #4 by order of the court dated (specify) 12/23/2019 [No Sch 2 Items]. Please explain the order of the court dated 12/23/2019 appointed Karen Ann Simons as referee. 4) There is no attached detailed schedules of receipts. 1 total amount for all the receipts is not detailed and must be amended. When an account has been waived, the report must contain the information required by rule 7.550. If the report is accompanied by a request for statutory commissions or fees, the basis for their computation must be included in the petition substantially in the form provided in (a). Notwithstanding the waiver of account, if the petition and report requests statutory commissions or fees based on any amount other than the amount of the Inventory and Appraisal, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705]. 5) Please explain why the calculation of statutory fees allege $102.10 in additional interest accrued when the account alleges $90.86 in interest. 6) Please explain the calculation of statutory fees. The Final I&A alleges $318,591.62. The account alleges $90.86 in interest. However, the calculation alleges $102.10 in interest. Please explain how $319,693.72 is the basis for the calculations of statutory compensation. 7) The petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice to the Franchise Tax Board was mailed pursuant to Probate Code 9202(c). [LRSF 14.36 I. 2)] 8) Please explain who High E. Macbeth III is and why High E. Macbeth III listed as an heir of Decedent on Exhibit C and person entitled to notice on Exhibit D. 9) The prayer on page 11 of the petition does not explain how the estate is to be distributed. The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. 10) The proposed order must match what is requested in the petition. The proposed order page 5 #4 is not in the prayer of the petition. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/12/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HARRY JAMES OWENS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The filing fees ($510) were waived in the decedent's conservatorship case. No reimbursement has been made to the Court from the decedent's estate. 2) The administrator's attorney requests $10,752.50 for her extraordinary services to the estate, including the services associated with the decedent's conservatorship case. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WING H. LOUIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/18/2021. Second contiuance same issues. 1) Declination of Mamie Louie to Serve as Executor; Nomination of Cory Louie includes text written in a foreign language. Whenever an instrument written in a foreign language is offered for probate or attached as an exhibit to a pleading, it must always be accompanied by a copy translated into English. All translations must be accompanied by a declaration setting forth the translator's qualifications and/or credentials. [LRSF 14.15]. 2) The bank statements attached to the petition show the accounts titled as Wing Louie POD Cory Louie. Please explain if the accounts are pay on death accounts and who the beneficiary on the account is. If all the alleged assets are pay on death accounts, please explain why a petition for probate has been filed. 3) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order for probate (DE-140) to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 4) Petitioner filed a Final I&A prior to being appointed. The Final I&A does not have any required attachments. (Probate Code 8800 et seq.) An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/04/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IGNACIO JIMENEZ PLASCENCIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/18/2021. Same issues. Continued to allow time to process e-filings. Issues: 1) Petitioner must file the original Will. [Probate Code 8200]. 2) A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/04/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KIEU DUONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/1/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. No proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7 A., B., and C., and 14.60 E.] No proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate has been filed. No proof of publication has been filed. No Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities has been filed yet. No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number has been filed yet. A cursory review of the petition online indicates that it is mostly incomplete and has inappropriate allegations (such as answering items 2b(1) and 3g(1) when the petitioner apparently seeks appointment as an administrator). Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (probably a fully amended petition) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/22/21. (If the estate is estimated at just $60,000, it is unclear why a formal estate is being opened.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGARET ROSE HOPPS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/1/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. No proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7 A., B., and C., and 14.60 E.] No proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate has been filed. No proof of publication has been filed. No Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities has been filed yet. No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number has been filed yet. A cursory review of the petition online indicates that much of it is incomplete or has inappropriate allegations. Among many other issues, the petitioner is missing from item 8 of the petition, and it is unclear who the person listed in item 8 is (his relationship to the decedent is not indicated). If the petitioner is a grandson, item 6f is not the first box that applies, and the petitioner's parent, who is/was the child of the decedent, must be identified. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (probably a fully amended petition) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/22/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
